---
title: "Installation & Setup"
---

This page covers the full installation and configuration process for the freely-walking optomotor system. For a brief prerequisites checklist, see the [Quickstart](freely_walking_quickstart.qmd) page. For hardware and arena specifications, see the [Rig Specs](freely-walking-rig-specs.qmd) page.

## System requirements

The system runs across three computers, each with a specific role:

| Computer | OS | Role |
|:---------|:---|:-----|
| Acquisition rig | Windows | Runs protocols, records video |
| Processing machine | Windows | Automated tracking & processing |
| Analysis computer | Mac / any | Manual analysis, plotting, dashboard |
: {.bordered}


## Software dependencies

### 1. freely-walking-optomotor repository

Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/leburnett/freely-walking-optomotor.git
```

### 2. MATLAB toolboxes

The following MATLAB toolboxes are required on all computers:

- [ ] [Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox](https://mathworks.com/products/statistics.html)
- [ ] [Image Processing Toolbox](https://mathworks.com/products/image.html)
- [ ] [Circular Statistics Toolbox](https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/fileexchange/10676-circular-statistics-toolbox-directional-statistics) (from MATLAB File Exchange, by Philipp Berens)

### 3. Acquisition software

Install on the **acquisition rig**:

- [ ] [BIAS](http://stuff.iorodeo.com/notes/bias/) (SimpleBiasCameraInterface) — for video acquisition
- [ ] [G4_Display_Tools](https://github.com/JaneliaSciComp/G4_Display_Tools) — provides the `panel_com` functions for LED arena control

### 4. Processing software

Install on the **processing machine**:

- [ ] [FlyTracker](https://github.com/kristinbranson/FlyTracker) — for post-experiment tracking
- [ ] [JAABA](https://github.com/kristinbranson/JAABA) — for generating stimulus videos with fly trajectories


## Configuration

### Step 1 — Edit the config files

**MATLAB** — edit `config/get_config.m` (one line):

```matlab
cfg.project_root = '/path/to/your_data_root';
```

**Python** — edit `config/config.py` (one line):

```python
PROJECT_ROOT = Path("/path/to/your_data_root")
```

Both files contain commented examples for Windows and Mac. The required data subdirectories (`DATA/`, `results/`, `figures/`) are created automatically the first time `get_config()` or `config.py` is called.

### Step 2 — Add MATLAB paths

Run `setup_path.m` once per MATLAB session (or add it to your `startup.m`):

```matlab
run('/path/to/freely-walking-optomotor/setup_path.m')
```

This adds all `src/` subdirectories to the MATLAB path so that functions like `get_config()`, `process_freely_walking_data()`, and the protocol helper functions are available.

### Step 3 — Install the Python environment (optional)

::: {.callout-note}
The Python environment is **not** required to run experiments — experiments are run entirely in MATLAB using protocol scripts (e.g. `protocol_27.m`). Python is only needed for the [automated processing pipeline](freely_walking_pipeline.qmd) scripts (`monitor_and_copy`, `monitor_and_track`, `daily_processing`) and the [Dashboard](freely_walking_dashboard.qmd).
:::

```bash
cd freely-walking-optomotor/python/freely-walking-python
pixi install
```

This installs all Python dependencies (numpy, pandas, scipy, dash, etc.) into an isolated environment managed by [pixi](https://pixi.sh).


## Verifying your setup

Run through this checklist to confirm everything is working:

- [ ] **Repository:** The `freely-walking-optomotor` folder exists and contains `config/get_config.m`
- [ ] **MATLAB path:** Type `which get_config` in the MATLAB Command Window — it should return the path to the file
- [ ] **Toolboxes:** Type `which circ_mean` — it should return a path (Circular Statistics Toolbox)
- [ ] **Config:** Run `cfg = get_config()` and verify that `cfg.project_root` points to your data folder — the `DATA/`, `results/`, and `figures/` subdirectories should be created automatically


## Next steps

Once your environment is set up:

1. Read the [Quickstart](freely_walking_quickstart.qmd) to run your first experiment
2. See [Configuration & Paths](freely_walking_configuration.qmd) for the full config file reference and data flow details
3. See the [Pipeline](freely_walking_pipeline.qmd) page to understand the automated processing pipeline
